chapter_title.ar: بَابُ السَّعْيِ بَيْنَ الصَّفَا وَ الْمَرْوَةِ وَ مَا يُقَالُ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 142 – The Sa’ee between Al-Safa and Al-Marwa and what is to be said during it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/72)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/72)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَا مِنْ بُقْعَةٍ أَحَبَّ إِلَى اللَّهِ مِنَ الْمَسْعَى لِأَنَّهُ يُذِلُّ فِيهَا كُلَّ جَبَّارٍ .
وَ رُوِيَ أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ لِمَ جُعِلَ السَّعْيُ فَقَالَ مَذَلَّةً لِلْجَبَّارِينَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Aslam, from Yunus, from Abu Baseer who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘There is none from an area more Beloved to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> than the (area of) Sa’ee, because therein every tyrant is humbled’.
And it is reported that he<sup>asws</sup> was asked , ‘Why was the Sa’ee made to be?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘To humble the tyrants’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn Aslam from Yunus from abu Basir who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘No piece of land is more beloved to Allah than the area for walking between al-Safa’ and al-Marwah; this area humbles every tyrant.’ It is narrated that in answer to the question, ‘Why it “al-Sa‘y,” is there it was said, ‘It is called as such because it humbles the tyrants.’”
